DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT HARRAH'S SOUTH SHORE CORPORATION DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT

No appearance. The court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT HARRAH'S SOUTH SHORE CORPORATION DEMURRER to FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Harrah's South Shore Corporation ("HSS")'s demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

HSS argues that Plaintiff Vera Illing's first cause of action for negligence and second cause of action for "intentional tort" fail under CCP 430.10(e) and are barred because Illing failed to seek administrative remedies in California. Mot. 3:7-15. Illing argues that HSS "is not actually a functioning independent legal entity" and that the employees' conduct which created an alleged hostile work environment "must be imputed to the Harvey Defendants." Opp., 3:8-26. [The Harvey defendants were dismissed on jurisdictional grounds (Reply, 2:11-12)].

The operative complaint and its attachments only allege employment related duties:
Defendant engaged in such wrongful employment practices that working conditions were so intolerable as to amount to firing. With knowledge that a customer was dangerous and violent, defendant allowed the customer access to plaintiff's work area and create a hostile work environment. Defendant's work practices were reckless with a conscious disregard for plaintiff's safety.

Complaint (emphasis added).

Each of the defendants negligently and intentionally employed or supervised "hosts" whose jobs was to accompany "V.I.P." guests to the Harrah's Diamond Lounge and were specifically instructed to ignore disorderly disruptive, profane, drunk, and potentially violent conduct of "V.I.P." guests and were not allowed to report such conduct to defendants' security employees. Hosts employed or supervised by each of the defendants accompanied potentially dangerous guests to the Harrah's Diamond Lounge and the hotel/casino premises where plaintiff was subjected to the hostile work environment referred to herein.

Attachments A+ B, pertaining to HSS (emphasis added).

HSS never employed Illing. MPA, 9:1-2. Plaintiff does not allege otherwise. Thus it is clear that although the operative complaint cities only employment-related duties (in connection with a "hostile work environment"), HSS could not have owed Illing any employment related duties.

Illing argues that doctrines of vicarious liability including respondeat superior impute the liability of others (such as Harrah's) who were employers, to HSS as an agent. The argument is incoherent. These doctrines, as HSS notes in its papers, can be used to impute liability from the agent (e.g. HSS) to a principal (e.g. Harrah's), not the other way around. Plaintiff's counsel seems to note the way this works in the Opposition, 2:7-8. See generally, e.g., Presbyterian Camp & Conference Centers, Inc. v. Superior Court (2021) 12 Cal.5th 493, 502; Marez v. Lyft, Inc. (2020) 48 Cal.App.5th 569, 577. So it doesn't help to argue that HSS's employees were employees of Harrah's (see Oppos. at 3:13 et seq.), unless Illing was arguing that Harrah's was liable for the acts of HSS-but plaintiff here is not arguing for Harrah's liability but of that of HSS.

HSS argues that Illing's second cause of action for intentional tort fails to state sufficient facts under CCP 430.10(e), because she failed to seek administrative remedies in California, and is uncertain under CCP 430.10(f). Mtn., 3:8-17. It is unnecessary to address these arguments.

The complaint has been repeatedly amended, including by way of "Doe" amendments and various attachments. Plaintiff has not sought leave to amend or explained how an amendment could fix the problems with the operative pleading. Thus leave to amend is not granted.

Order signed after hearing.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (302/CK)

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT HARRAH'S SOUTH SHORE CORPORATION DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer to Plaintiff Vera Illing's first amended complaint is ordered off calendar. Per CCP sec. 916(a) Plaintiff moves to stay this action pending its appeal of this Court's November 9, 2021, Order granting specially appearing defendants Harvey's Tahoe Management Company, Inc. and Harvey's Tahoe Management Company, LLC (Harveys)'s motion to quash service of summons for lack of jurisdiction. CCP sec. 916(a) provides, in pertinent part, "the perfecting of an appeal stays proceedings in the trial court upon the judgment or order appealed from or upon the matters embraced therein or affected thereby . . ." The purpose of the automatic stay is to protect the Court of Appeal's jurisdiction, not prevent inconvenience to the litigants. (Henry M. Lee Law Corp. v. Superior Court (2012) 204 Cal.App.4th 1375, 1383 ["The purpose of the automatic stay is to protect the appellate court's jurisdiction by preserving the status quo and preventing the trial court from undermining or otherwise affecting the effectiveness of the appeal by altering the appealed judgment or order."]; LAOSD Asbestos cases (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 862, 872 ["The function of the automatic stay rule is ''to protect the appellate court's jurisdiction by preserving the status quo until the appeal is decided.'].) Regardless, Defendant does not contest Plaintiff's request to stay.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.
